sanskrit:
त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।
त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे॥
transliteration:
tvam akṣaraṃ paramaṃ veditavyaṃ tvam asya viśvasya paraṃ nidhānam |
tvam avyayaḥ śāśvata-dharma-goptā sanātanas tvaṃ puruṣo mato me ||
literal_meaning: You are the imperishable supreme object to be known; You are the ultimate resting-place of this universe; You are the inexhaustible, eternal guardian of dharma, the everlasting Person—this is my conviction.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s ego has collapsed; in awe he recognizes Krishna as the unchanging ground of consciousness that holds all mental contents, the archetypal Self that regulates inner order and meaning.
psychological_state: Peak transpersonal experience: ego boundaries dissolve, revealing a stable, deathless center that silently orchestrates psychic life.
trigger_scenario: A therapist, mid-session, suddenly sees the client’s entire narrative as a flickering movie on the unbroken screen of awareness—an embodied glimpse that ‘I am not my story, I am the space in which it unfolds.’
anchor_text: Rest in the changeless Self that silently sustains all thoughts and actions.
